title: Public policy and economic growth
description: Practice what you've learned about growth policies in this exercise.

assessment_items.question_content:
The economy of Marthaland decreased tax rates on businesses and households, which has changed the savings behavior of private households.

**What happens to household savings and the real interest rate in the short run, and potential output in the long run?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Household saving increases; the real interest rate decreases, and potential output increases.
Household saving decreases; the real interest rate increases, and potential output decreases.
Household saving increases; the real interest rate decreases, and potential output is unaffected.
Household saving increases; the real interest rate decreases, and potential output decreases.
Household saving decreases; the real interest rate decreases, and potential output increases.

assessment_items.question_content:
Higher taxes have reduced disposable income in Fredland. 

**What happens to household savings and the real interest rate in the short run, and potential output in the long run?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Household saving decreases; the real interest rate increases, and potential output decreases.
Household saving increases; the real interest rate decreases, and potential output increases.
Household saving increases; the real interest rate decreases, and potential output is unaffected.
Household saving decreases; the real interest rate decreases, and potential output increases.
Household saving decreases; the real interest rate decreases, and potential output increases.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following policies is most likely to increase potential output in the long run?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Policies that encourage more saving for retirement
Policies that encourage more household spending.
Taxes on business investment.
Taxes on the gains from the sale of assets.
Weakening protections on intellectual property.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is the most likely effect of a government policy that leads to more development of technology used by firms throughout an economy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a positive supply shock and higher growth rate
a negative demand shock and a lower price level
a positive supply shock and a higher price level
a negative supply shock and a higher price level
a positive demand shock and a lower growth rate

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is LEAST likely to be associated with a higher rate of economic growth?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: higher consumption
strong protection of property rights
encouraging the replenishment of natural resources
taxes on savings and investment
policies that discourage immigration

assessment_items.question_content:
**What combination of income taxes, real interest rates, and investment spending is associated with lower rates of economic growth?**



[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Higher taxes, higher real interest rate, and lower investment spending
lower taxes, lower real interest rate, and higher investment spending
Higher taxes, lower real interest rate, and higher investment spending
Lower taxes, lower real interest rate, and lower investment spending

assessment_items.question_content:
The economy of Atlantis has experienced a sustained increase in real GDP per capita for many years. 

**Which of the following is NOT a likely reason for this increase?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: An accelerating depreciation rate of capital
More health and sanitation programs
Government spending on infrastructure projects
More capital per worker
An acceleration in the rate of technological change

assessment_items.question_content:
Winterfell and Dorne have no restrictions on the flow of financial capital. An increase in business optimism in Dorne has led to a higher real interest rate in Dorne.

**What effect does this have on real interest rates and long-run economic growth in Winterfell?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Higher interest rates and lower economic growth
Higher interest rates and higher economic growth
Neither interest rates nor economic growth in Winterfell are affected.
Lower interest rates and higher economic growth
Lower interest rates and lower economic growth

assessment_items.question_content:
Assume that the kingdoms of Florin and Guilder have no restrictions on the flow of financial assets. A dramatic increase in military spending in Florin has lead to a large budget deficit there.

**What effect does this have on real interest rates and long-run economic growth in Guilder?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Higher interest rates and lower economic growth.
Higher interest rates and higher economic growth
Neither interest rates nor economic growth in Guilder are affected.
Lower interest rates and higher economic growth
Lower interest rates and lower economic growth.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is most likely to lead to higher economic growth?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: High levels of infrastructure development.
Low levels of immigration.
High levels of taxation.
Low levels of diffusion of technology.
High levels of distrust in the banking system.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is LEAST likely to lead to higher levels of economic growth?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: low levels of foreign investment
high levels of immigration
low government borrowing
low savings rates
low investment demand

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is most likely to lead to higher levels of economic growth?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: an increase in inflows of foreign savings.
a decrease in labor force participation rates.
high government deficits
less retirement savings.
less business optimism
